response: {"purpose":"Figure 4b demonstrates the effectiveness of the paired size-matched input (SMInput) control in eCLIP by showing the percentage of CLIPper-identified binding clusters that are genuinely enriched over background across different genomic regions, spanning 102 eCLIP experiments conducted in biological duplicate in K562 and HepG2 cells. By comparing cluster enrichment against the SMInput rather than a simple read-count threshold, this panel highlights the RNA-centric normalization strategy that distinguishes true protein–RNA interactions from signals arising merely from abundant transcripts. This matters because it validates that the SMInput approach reliably separates authentic RBP binding sites from background, even in regions occupied by highly expressed noncoding RNAs where false positives would otherwise be problematic.","title":"SMInput normalization enriches authentic RBP clusters across genomic regions"}
